properties.remark: A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM PASSING THROUGH THE AREA.

SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS ASSOCIATED WITH A STRONG LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS...48 MPH...FROM THE WEST...WAS RECORDED AT THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION DURING THE PASSAGE OF A SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...43 MPH...FROM THE WEST-NORTHWEST...WAS RECORDED AT A UNIVERSITY OF SOUTH FLORIDA PLATFORM IN NORTHWEST FLORIDA BAY DURING THE PASSAGE OF A SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 56 KNOTS...64 MPH...FROM THE WEST-SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION DURING THE PASSAGE OF A SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 47 KNOTS...54 MPH...FROM THE WEST-SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE LONG KEY C-MAN STATION DURING THE PASSAGE OF A SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS...44 MPH...FROM THE WEST...WAS RECORDED AT THE ISLAMORADA FIRE RESCUE DEPARTMENT DURING THE PASSAGE OF A SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS...46 MPH...FROM THE WEST...WAS RECORDED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION AS A SQUALL LINE MOVED THROUGH.
A WIND GUST OF 44 KNOTS...51 MPH...FROM THE WEST-NORTHWEST...WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION DURING THE PASSAGE OF A SQUALL LINE.

A WEST-NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 47 KTS...OR 54 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 345 PM EST. THIS GUST WAS FROM A PASSING THUNDERSTORM ASSOCIATED WITH AN APPROACHING COLD FRONT.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...OR 45 MPH...FROM THE WEST WAS REPORTED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT C-MAN PLATFORM. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS OR 48 MPH OUT OF THE SOUTH WAS RECORDED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION AS A THUNDERSTORM PASSED OVERHEAD.

A WEST-SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 35 KTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 1126 AM EDT. THIS THUNDERSTORM GUST WAS FROM A PASSING SQUALL LINE...OUT AHEAD OF AN APPROACHING COLD FRONT.

A SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 38 KTS...OR 44 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 1245 PM EDT. THIS THUNDERSTORM GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE...OUT AHEAD OF AN APPROACHING COLD FRONT.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHWEST WAS REPORTED AT THE LONG KEY LIGHT C-MAN PLATFORM. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS FROM THE NORTH WAS MEASURED AT THE STATION AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY.
A WIND GUST OF 41 MPH...OR 36 KNOTS...WAS REPORTED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.

AT 1122 AM EDT...AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT EXTENDING HALF-WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE WATER SURFACE. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT OBSERVED. MOVEMENT WAS SLOWLY TO THE SOUTHWEST AND THE SPOUT DISSIPATED AT 1136 AM. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 15 MINUTES.
WFO MIAMI RELAYED REPORT FROM CWSU MIAMI OF A REPORTED WATERSPOUT REPORTED BY PILOT.

MEMEBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED THAT STORMS PASSING OVER JEWFISH CREEK CAUSED SOME MINOR DAMAGE. WINDS INCREASED TO NEAR 45 MPH OUT OF THE SOUTHWEST AROUND 1230 PM THEN QUICKLY SHIFTED NORTHWEST GUSTING TO NEAR 60 MPH, THEN SHIFTED WEST AS THE STORM PASSED WITHIN 30 MINUTES OF ONSET. IMPACTS INCLUDED TRASH CANS BLOWN ACROSS PROPERTY AND A MAILBOX DISTROYED.

A TRAINED SPOTTER ON CUDJOE KEY REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 MILES TO THE SOUTH-SOUTHEAST. THE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED ABOUT HALFWAY TO THREE-QUARTERS OF THE WAY TO THE SURFACE AND THEN QUICKLY DISSIPATED. DURATION ESTIMATED TO BE ABOUT 3 MINUTES.

AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED 4 FUNNEL CLOUDS EXTENDING ONE QUARTER OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE. OBSERVATION WAS MADE WHILE LOOKING WESTWARD FROM MM 15 ALONG U.S. HIGHWAY 1. NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED THREE FUNNEL CLOUDS LOOKING NORTHWARD FROM THE RADIOSONDE TOWER AT WFO KEY WEST. ONE FUNNEL CLOUD WAS BROAD IN WIDTH WHILE TWO OTHER FUNNEL CLOUDS WERE NARROW. MOVEMENT WAS SOUTHWESTWARD AROUND 15 KNOTS. EVENT DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 12 MINUTES.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A SHORT LIVED WATERSPOUT SOUTHEAST OF KEY WEST. FUNNEL EXTENDED A QUARTER OF THE WAY TO THE SEA SURFACE WITH NO VISIBLE SPRAY RING. DURATION 4 MINUTES.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WELL DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT SOUTH SOUTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT EXTENDED TO THE SEA SURFACE AND HAD A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. DURATION 5 MINUTES.

OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A FUNNEL CLOUD THAT EXTENDED APPROXIMATELY ONE QUARTER OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE. NO SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED. LOCATION WAS ABOUT 3 MILES NORTHEAST OF BOCA CHICA NAVAL AIR STATION. EVENT DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.

OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDING HALF WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE. LOCATION WAS 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF MALLORY SQUARE IN OLD TOWN KEY WEST. THE FUNNEL WAS THIN IN WIDTH. NO SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED. EVENT DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 5 MINUTES.
A NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT LOCATED ABOUT 5 MILES NORTH OF BIG PINE KEY. ANY EVIDENCE OF A SPRAY RING WAS NOT EVIDENT DUE TO AN OBSTRUCTED VIEW OF THE HORIZON.
41 KNOT WIND GUST AT SENSOR HEIGHT OF 149 FEET
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 10 MILES SOUTH OF MILE MARKER 72. THIS WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF SHOWERS FORMING ACROSS THE MIDDLE KEYS.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE EAST NORTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT CMAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING CLUSTER OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 4 MILES NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED JUST EAST OF A SHOWER EMBEDDED IN A WEST TO EAST ORIENTED CUMULUS CLOUD LINE NORTHWEST OF THE LOWER KEYS.
THE COAST GUARD REPORTED A WATERPOUT 2 MILES SOUTH OF BOOT KEY OR ONE HALF MILE SOUTH OF THE 7 MILE BRIDGE. THIS WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A DEVELOPING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.

A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING DEVELOPED OVER HAWK CHANNEL ABOUT 4 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHEAST OF SOMBRERO BEACH AND MOVED NORTHWESTWARD FOR A FEW MILES BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST SOUTH OF THE EAST END OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE. DURATION 21 MINUTES.
SE 34 KT WIND FROM THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY.

THREE WATERSPOUTS WERE REPORTED BY TRAIN SPOTTERS APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF PIDGEON KEY. THESE WATERSPOUTS WERE ASSOCIATED WITH SHOWERS THAT FORMED ALONG AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT 4 MILES NORTHWEST OF FOUNDERS PARK IN ISLAMORADA. THIS WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH DEVELOPING CONVECTION NORTH OF ISLAMORADA.
A WIND GUST OF 48 KNOTS...OR 55 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS RECORDED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT CMAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING CLUSTER OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES SOUTH OF BOCA CHICA. THIS WATERSPOUT AS ASSOCIATED WITH A DEVELOPING SHOWER.
35 KNOT WIND GUST MEASURED AT SAND KEY LIGHT
39 KNOT WIND GUST AT SAND KEY LIGHT
A WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS...OR 46 MPH...FROM THE EAST SOUTHEAST WAS REPORTED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT CMAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...OR 45 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHEAST WAS MEASURED BY AN OFFSHORE TOWER IN NORTHWEST FLORIDA BAY ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS...OR 44 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHEAST WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT MOLASSES REEF LIGHT IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.

A WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS...OR 46 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHEAST WAS MEASURED AT THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
AN EAST-SOUTHEAST WIND GUST OF 34 KTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT AT 956 AM EDT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...OR 45 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS REPORTED BY THE PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT CMAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT ASOS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING CLUSTER OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING CLUSTER OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH FAST MOVING SHOWERS.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 7 MILES SOUTHEAST OF THE NAVAL AIR STATION ON BOCA CHICA KEY. THE VISIBLE FUNNEL CLOUD EXTENDED HALF WAY TO THE SEA SURFACE FROM CLOUD BASE AND WAS LOCATED ADJACENT TO A CLUSTER OF SHOWERS. MOVEMENT WAS TOWARD THE NORTHWEST. DURATION 13 MINUTES.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A THIN WATERSPOUT EAST OF PLANTATION KEY MOVING WSW.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WELL DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1 MILE OFFSHORE MOVING WSW.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A PENCIL THIN WATERSPOUT ABOUT 3 MILES SOUTH OF THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT. DURATION OF SPOUT 6 MINUTES.
THREE WATERSPOUTS OCCURING SIMULTANEOUSLY WERE REPORTED BY A TRAINED WEATHER OBSERVER AT THE NAVAL AIR STATION ON BOCA CHICA KEY ABOUT 7 MILES NORTH OF THE AIRFIELD. DURATION 16 MINUTES.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED TWO WATERSPOUTS NORTH OF THE SHERRIFS SUBSTATION AT CUDJOE KEY. ONE OF THE WATERPOUSTS HAD A SPRAY RING AND WAS VISIBILE ALL THE WAY TO THE WATER.
A PILOT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 35 MILES NORTH NORTHEAST OF KEY WEST. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A PILOT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 40 MILES NORTH NORTHEAST OF KEY WEST. DURATION UNKNOWN.

A NORTHERLY WIND GUST OF 37 KTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 820 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM PASSING OVER THE SITE TO THE SOUTHWEST.
THE OBSERVER AT KEY WEST AIRPORT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF THE AIRPORT. THIS WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A BOUNDARY MOVING IN FROM THE NORTHWEST.
WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS MEASURED AT THE C-MAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY.
A WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS...OR 44 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHEAST WAS MEASURED BY THE ASOS AT THE KEY WEST NAVAL AIR STATION ON BOCA CHICA...AS AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY MOVED THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS MEASURED BY THE CITIZEN WEATHER OBSERVER PROGRAM INSTRUMENT LOCATED ON CUDJOE KEY.
A WIND GUST OF 46 KNOTS...OR 53 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHWEST WAS MEASURED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION...AS AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY MOVED THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS...OR 47 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS MEASURED AT THE NORTHWEST FLORIDA BAY OFFSHORE MESONET SITE. THIS OCCURRED AS AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY WAS PASSING THROUGH THE AREA.

A RETIRED AIR TRAFFIC CONTROLER SPOTTED A WATERSPOUT MOVING ON SHORE NEAR MILE MARKER 102 IN THE WINSTON WATERWAYS COMMUNITY IN KEY LARGO. DAMAGE INCLUDED A FEW TREE BRANCHES DOWNED AND SOME PATIO FURNITURE TOSSED. A MINOR POWER OUTAGE OCCURRED NEARBY AND 5 CUSTOMERS LOST POWER BRIEFLY. THE EF-0 TORNADO/WATERSPOUT LASTED ONLY A FEW SECONDS WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND OF 50 MPH, WHILE TRAVELING BETWEEN 300 AND 500 FEET BEFORE DISSIPATING.

A NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 35 KTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 531 AM EDT. THIS GUST WAS IN ASSOCIATION WITH A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS PROGRESSING NORTHEASTWARD UP THE KEYS.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY FLORIDA FISH AND WILDLIFE DISPATCH ON THE BAYSIDE OF LONG KEY BRIDGE. DURATION UNKNOWN.
THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE EAST-SOUTHEAST AS A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY PASSED THROUGH THE AREA.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NORTH NORTHWEST OF CUDJOE KEY. THE VISIBLE FUNNEL EXTENDED THREE QUARTERS OF THE WAY TO THE WATER SURFACE. DURATION 4 MINUTES.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS NORTH OF FLEMING KEY. THE WATERSPOUTS FORMED WITHIN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE NORTH OF THE LOWER KEYS. DURATION UNKNOWN.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NORTH OF KEY WEST WITHIN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE. DURATION UNKNOWN.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE EAST AS THUNDERSTORMS MOVING WEST-NORTHWESTWARD PASSED THROUGH THE AREA.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 MILES SOUTH OF RAMROD KEY NEAR A LINE OF WESTWARD MOVING SHOWERS. DURATION 7 MINUTES.

AT 345 PM EDT A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC SPOTTED A WATERSPOUT NEAR MILE MARKER 102 OF US 1 OVER THE WATERS OF FLORIDA BAY. IT WAS VISIBLE FOR APPROXIMATELY 4 MINUTES...REACHING THE SURFACE FOR ABOUT 2 MINUTES. AS OF 350 PM IT WAS OBSCURED BY HEAVY RAINFALL.
A SOUTHEAST WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...45 MPH...WAS RECORDED AT THE AUTOMATED WEATHER STATION AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHTHOUSE DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
A SOUTHEAST WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...45 MPH...WAS RECORDED AT THE AUTOMATED WEATHER STATION AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHTHOUSE DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
NAS BOCA CHICA OBSERVER CALL AT 659 PM OBSERVING A FUNNEL CLOUD APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE WEST OF KEY WEST. THIS WAS VERIFIED BY TWO NWS EMPLOYEES AT 703 PM.
A WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS...OR 46 MPH... FROM THE NORTH-NORTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH NEARBY THUNDERSTORM ACTIVITY.
TWO WATER SPOUTS WERE OBSERVED 10 MILES SOUTH OF DIXIE SHOALS BY A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER.
A TRAINED OBSERVER AT THE NAVAL AIR STATION ON BOCA CHICA KEY REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 2 MILES NORTH OF THE AIRFIELD. THE VISIBLE FUNNEL EXTENDED TO THE WATER SURFACE. DURATION 16 MINUTES.

A SOUTH-SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 41 KTS...OR 47 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 908 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A ROBUST LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS ORIENTED NW-SE AND MOVING TO THE NORTH-NORTHEAST.

A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...43 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH- SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION. ANEMOMETER HEIGHT 159 FEET ABOVE SEA LEVEL. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A BAND OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS MOVING NORTH-NORTHEASTWARD THROUGH THE AREA.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER/STORM CHASER REPORTED MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS OCCURING WITHIN A SW-NE ORIENTED CLOUD BAND 5 MILES SOUTH OF KEY COLONY BEACH IN THE MIDDLE KEYS. NO VISIBLE SPRAY RING WAS EVIDENT. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS...OR 48 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS REPORTED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT CMAN STATION IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS ABOUT 5 MILES SOUTH OF MARATHON, NEAR SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. DURATION UNKNOWN.
